TaskContentRequest.description:
Task content request. The content structure varies depending the task type. If the variables.type is Sensitive("4"), the variables.value should be encrypted by RIPEMD160 algorithm. The variable enum is 
 enum EnumVariableType {
        Unknown("-1"),
        Reserved("0"),
        Text("1"),
        Numerical("2"),
        Date("3"),
        Sensitive("4"),
        DateTime("5"),
        SystemPrompt("4106"),
        TxnRowProvenance("32769");
}For script task, the content is {  "objectId": "stringScriptObjId",  "variables": [    {"id": "id1", "value": "string", "type": "2"},    {"id": "id2", "value": "string2", "type": "4"},}  ]}. For workflow task, the content is {  "objectId": "stringWorkflowObjId",  "versionId": "string",  "entryProcessId": "string",  "variables": [    { "id": "idA", "value": "stringA", "type": "2"},    { "id": "idB", "value": "stringB","type": "4"}  ],  "description": "string",  "expiredOn": 0}.

ms-Cube.description:
Object that specifies an _Intelligent Cube defintion_.
An Intelligent Cube is a set of data that can be shared as a single in-memory copy, among many different reports created by multiple users. Rather than returning data from the data warehouse for a single report, you can return sets of data from your data warehouse and save them directly to Intelligence Server memory. The reports accessing Intelligent Cubes can use all of the OLAP Services features for analysis and reporting purposes.

The cube defintion describes what template units needs to be included into the cube, such as attributes, metrics. 

The template definition describes what you data you want to include into the cube. You are able to add attributes, metrics, and so on, in rows, columns, or pageBys. The cube template could be either standalone or embedded to the cube. When the template is embedded, the `rows`, `columns`, and `pageBy` could be edited. When the cube is using a standalone object. The `standaloneTemplate` contains the reference. If you want to see the defintion of the standalone template, you can call **GET** _/model/templates/{TemplateId}_. The `standaloneTemplate` is read-only and not editable. We encourage you to use embedded templates. As for now, you can only converting a standalone template to embedded by defining the `rows`, `columns`, and `pageBy`.

The model doesn't contain the real data from the data warehouse. You need to publish the cube to get the real data.

filter.title: Cube Filter
filter.description: By defining a cube filter, the data will be filtered accordingly when you publish your data. You can also define a bunch of options to control the behavior of cube regarding data and publishing. If you create a filter on an Intelligent Cube, any data that is restricted from the Intelligent Cube is not available for any reports that connect to the Intelligent Cube. While this can help reduce the size of the Intelligent Cube, it also reduces the amount of data available in the Intelligent Cube. The cube filter could be either standalone or embedded. When the cube filter is standalone, you will see `standaloneFilter` in the `filter` property. The `standaloneFilter` property is read-only. We encourage the users to use a cube embedded filter. Currently, you can not modify a cube by assigning a `standaloneFilter`.

propertyCount.description: total number of properties returned for a particular object

dynamicSqlPreview.description: Contains information about all the Properties that can be used in the dynamic sql preview.

ms-IncrementalRefreshReport.title: Incremental Refresh Report Definition
ms-IncrementalRefreshReport.description:
Object that specifies an _Incremental Refresh Report defintion_.
An Incremental Refresh Report is a report to partially refresh an Intelligence Cube's data. Rather than completely updating the whole Intelligence Cube's data, you can use Incremental Refresh Report to partially refresh Intelligence Cube's data with different mode.

The incremental refresh report defintion describes how to refresh an Intelligence Cube's data.

The `targetCube` reference specifies the Intelligence Cube to be refreshed. 

The `incrementType` and `refreshType` determines the mode to execute incremental refresh report and the way to update target Intelligence Cube data.

The template is only available for `report` type incremental refresh report. The template definition describes what you data you want to include into the incremental refresh report. You are able to add attributes, metrics, and so on, in rows, columns, or pageBys. The incremental refresh report template could be either standalone or embedded to the incremental refresh report. When the template is embedded, the `rows`, `columns`, and `pageBy` could be edited. When the incremental refresh report is using a standalone object. The `standaloneTemplate` contains the reference. If you want to see the defintion of the standalone template, you can call **GET** _/model/templates/{TemplateId}_. The `standaloneTemplate` is read-only and not editable. We encourage you to use embedded templates. As for now, you can only converting a standalone template to embedded by defining the `rows`, `columns`, and `pageBy`.

By defining a incremental refresh report filter, the incremental refresh report data will be filtered and the report data will be used to refresh Intelligence Cube's data with defined types.

The model doesn't contain the real data from the data warehouse.

isTimezoneAware.description:
Flag controlling whether this attribute is timezone aware. To enable this flag the attribute must contain a simple key form with
the dataType `timestamp`.
Setting this flag to true will also trigger a check to ensure that the attribute contains only one form and no relationships.
The `removeInvalidFields` can be used to force remove all additional forms/relationships.

If used, this comment may help to justify why the schema was locked.

ms-Lock.description:
An object that contains all of the information about the lock status of a schema.
If the schema is not locked then none of the other fields should be provided.

ms-EnumLockType.title: Lock Type
ms-EnumLockType.description:
A type of lock that can be placed on the schema.
* "absolute_individual": When placed, no one, including the administrator, can perform changes on the schema objects. The purpose of this lock is to prevent accidental modification to the schema in a project.
* "exclusive_constituent": Not used by this API. When the changeset is created with "schemaEdit" = `true`, an "exclusive_constituent" lock is placed on schema. It is a lock exclusive to the changeset holding the lock; only that changeset can change the schema or its constituents (tables, attributes, etc.).
* "absolute_constituent: Not in use.

ms-EnumUpdateType.title: Update Type
ms-EnumUpdateType.description: Type of update for the schema.

startTime.description: The date/time at which the task execution started.

endTime.description: The date/time at which the task execution completed (or failed).

errors.description: List of errors if the task execution failed.

allOf.description:
Specify the datatype that the engine should use to store a intermediate values for this fact.

The model service will select an initial value for this property as a side-effect of
setting a first fact expression on some table.  It will assume that values of the fact should
be represented using the datatype deduced for the expression on its initial table.
The client may override this selection if it is not the correct datatype to use.

Observe that even if there is only one fact
expression (on multiple tables) it is possible that the expression has different data-types
on different tables.  So it is not possible for the model service to deduce an
unambiguously correct datatype for a fact.

entryLevel.description:
This is the _natural_ level (in other words a set of attributes) of the fact.

This property is not set by the architects.  Instead it is deduced by the platform by examining the
specification of the fact, and considering the attributes and the relationships between them.
Because it can only be only be computed by considering the schema as a whole, the value is not
automatically updated each time the fact is modified.  It will be correct following a _schema
update_ operation.

ms-Fact.description:
An object giving all of the definition information for a Fact object.

A fact object is a mapping from tuples of elements to a value.  It is stored in a data source.
The distinction between a _fact_ and a _metric_ is that the former is only available at those
levels for which an explicit computation is specified in the metadata.  In contrast metrics
can be aggregated (from fact data) to give values at other levels.

Note that some of the information described here is actually stored in the metadata as part of
other objects.

ms-GetTablesResponse.description: Tables information returned from API when getting a list of tables from changeset.

allOf.description:
MicroStrategy support mapping the table to more than one data source.
The first option is primary data source while the second option is second data source.

ms-ObjectInfoReference.title: Object Reference

isEmbedded.description:
If true indicates that the target object of this reference is embedded within this object.
Alternatively if this object is itself embedded, then it means that the target object is
embedded in the same container as this object.

If this field is omitted (as is usual) then the target is not embedded.

objects.

title.description: Major string displayed to a user when requesting an answer for this prompt

instruction.description: Minor string displayed to a user when requesting an answer for this prompt

expressionType.description: only useful when prompt type is expression prompt

ms-Prompt.description:
Object that specifies a _prompt_.
A prompt give user a chance to dynamicly select the data they want.

ms-Filter.title: Filter

allOf.description:
The filter definition written as an expression tree over predicate nodes.

We do not attempt to represent the entire filter using expression nodes.
Instead we use _predicate_ nodes as leaves of the main tree expression.
Most predicate nodes use a simple data structure.
These simple predicates correspond to the most common qualifications that are used in filters.
We also have a custom predicate which uses parse tokens to represent predicates that
we support but which cannot be described using the usual pattern.

It is valid for a filter expression to contain no nodes.  This is known as an _empty _filter_.
An empty filter implies that no restriction is made when applying the filter to a dataset.
A better name might have been a _universal filter_.

ms-Filter.description:
Object that specifies a _filter_.
A filter specifies a restriction that might be applied to a dataset when it is used in a computation.

A filter takes the form of an expression tree over data structures known as _predicates_.
Each predicate represents a single restriction on the available data.
The filter expression tree combines predicates together using logical operators.

ms-CustomGroup.title: Custom Group
ms-CustomGroup.description:
Object that specifies a _custom group_.

A custom group is a set of special filters that can be placed on a template, 
it is made up of an ordered collection of elements called custom group elements.

A custom group element's qualification takes the form of an expression tree over data structures known as _predicates_.
Each predicate represents a single restriction on the available data.
The custom group element expression tree combines predicates together using logical operators.

ms-DrillMap.title: Drill Map
ms-DrillMap.description:
Object that specifies a _drill map_

Drill map is a list of customized drill paths that used to override the default drilling behavior.
A drill map can be assocaited with attributes, consolidations, custom groups, templates, and report units.

ms-SecurityFilter.title: Security Filter

allOf.description:
The security filter definition written as an expression tree over predicate nodes.

We do not attempt to represent the entire security filter using expression nodes.
Instead we use _predicate_ nodes as leaves of the main tree expression.

Most predicate nodes use a simple data structure.
Security Filter support five types of predicates, including PredicateCustomExpression,PredicateJointElementList,
PredicateElementList, PredicateForm, PredicateFilter.
These simple predicates correspond to the most common qualifications that are used in security filters.

It is invalid for a security filter expression to contain no nodes.

topLevel.description:
The top level attribute list. Top and bottom range attributes place aggregation ceilings and floors on metrics used on a report.
If neither a top nor a bottom range attribute is specified, the security filter is applied to every level of analysis.

bottomLevel.description:
The bottom level attribute list. Top and bottom range attributes place aggregation ceilings and floors on metrics used on a report.
If neither a top nor a bottom range attribute is specified, the security filter is applied to every level of analysis.

ms-SecurityFilter.description:
Object that specifies a _security filter_.
A security filter can be assigned to a user or group to narrow the result set when they execute reports or browse elements.

A security filter takes the form of an expression tree over data structures known as _predicates_.
Each predicate represents a single restriction on the available data.
The security filter expression tree combines predicates together using logical operators.

ms-TypeObjectId.title: Object Id
ms-TypeObjectId.description:
A globally unique identifier used to distinguish between metadata objects within the same project.
It is possible for two metadata objects in different projects to have the same _Object Id_.

Objects that are automatically created as part of a project or configuration are given fixed
ids which makes it easy to identify them.

ms-InnerTemplate.title: Inner Template
ms-InnerTemplate.description:
Object that specifies a _cube tempate_. A cube template defines the layout of general categories of information in a cube. In a cube template, you specify the information that you want to retrieve from your data source, and the way that you want the data to be displayed in Grid view.

The units in the Cube template don't support the types of _custom_group_, _consolidation_, _prompt_ and _raw_unit_.

ms-XdaLogicalTable.title: free form sql logical table
ms-XdaLogicalTable.description:
Free form sql logical table definition contains "physicalTable", "attributes", "metrics" and "dataSource" fields:
"physicalTable": contains a list of columns and a sql expression field.
"attributes": defines a list of attributes, attribute forms and their mapping columns.
"metrics": defines a list of metrics and their mapping columns.
"dataSource": specifies the data warehouse used as data source.

ms-Expression.title: Expression

text.description:
Human readable description of the expression.  It is generated from the current specification
of the expression.

This string will appear similar to a parsable description of the expression in
the current user's locale.  It is intended to be used to allow a user to easily
distinguish between expressions.  But this string cannot actually be used as input to a
parser session because it does not contain hidden information about ambiguities in
the parse text.

Since this representation is not able to fully describe an expression, there is
no point in the client ever sending it to the service.

tokens.description:
Optional array, used if the expression is to be presented as a stream of tokens.

All types of expressions can be represented in the form of tokens, but some expressions with type predicate are yet not supported to be used in the request input for validation or modification, even when you are using the tokens returned from the service. For e.g. predicate_joint_element_list,  predicate_element_list, and some cases involving embedded objects. Expected errors while be thrown in such cases.

When this representation is used by the service we would expect that the tokens would either
completely parse the expression, or would describe an error in the parsing.  When this representation
is used by the client the tokens would either correspond to fresh text from the user (a `client` token)
or to parts of the text that the user has not edited (a `parsed` token).

ms-Expression.description:
A generic specification for a calculation stored within a metadata object.
The expression is represented as a tree over nodes.  Most internal nodes
(called _operator_ nodes) are defined by applying a function to the operator's child nodes.

Usually an expression must be non-empty.  But in a few cases, most notably a filter expression
it is valid for an expression to contain no nodes at all.  An expression is _empty_ if and only if
it does not have a `tree` property.

Alternatively the client may prefer to handle an expression as a list of tokens.
Each token represents part of the raw text of the expression, in some cases annotated
with additional information.

ms-ApplicableProperty.title: Applicable property object

name.description: Property name - a reference to the server object which appears in english only.

groupId.description: Id of the property group, used by client to group properties in folders.

groupName.description: Property group name used by the client to group properties in folders which can be internationalized.

maxValue.description: Shows maximum number of options that can be provided for the property.

displayType.description: Determines the way of displaying properties by caller.

displayName.description: Represents the display name of the property which can be internationalized.

showSqlPreview.description: Boolean value used to determine whether to show SQL preview or not.

ms-ApplicableProperty.description: Represents an applicable property where Key of a property is a pair in the format "[<propertySetName>].[<propertyName>]"

ms-ObjectInfoBrowse.title: Browse Object
ms-ObjectInfoBrowse.description:
Description of an object providing values for the most frequently used fields.

The member values shown here correspond to the fields that would be supplied when browsing a folder
that contains this object.  The intent is that the user is given enough information to select an
interesting object.

description.description:
User supplied description for this object.

It is possible that the metadata might contain translations for the object's description.
In that case the description will be given in the metadata locale of the end user.

dateCreated.description: The date/time at which this object was first saved into the metadata.

dateModified.description: The date/time at which this object was last saved into the metadata.

versionId.description: The version number this object is currently carrying.

path.description: The path of the object

acg.description:
Object access rights. It is a bit vector where bits are defined at
[EnumDSSXMLAccessRightFlags](https://lw.microstrategy.com/msdz/msdl/GARelease_Current/docs/ReferenceFiles/reference/com/microstrategy/webapi/EnumDSSXMLAccessRightFlags.html)

id.description:
Attribute Form ID

The ID can be:
* the object ID used in metadata.
* the object ID used in changeset, but not yet committed to metadata.

Note that Form ID is associated with the form cateogry used. Multiple forms that use the same category will have the same ID.
However, since no two forms within the same attribute can use the same category, no two forms will share an ID in the same attribute.

name.description:
The name of the attribute form set by the attribute.
Unlike `category`, which is the systemic name associated with each reusable form, this name is specific to the attribute using this form.

category.description:
The category of the attribute form.
Unlike `name`, The field is independent of the attribute using this form.
This field can only be set when creating a new form. Once a form is created, its category becomes non-mutable.
If not provided (or set as `None`) when an attribute is being created, a custom category will be automatically generated.

type.description: A read-only field indicating the type of this form. A custom form is created if its category is set to `None`.

isMutilingual.description:
A boolean field indicating whether this field is multilingual.
The default value(`false`) will be hidden from the response.
Any key form of the attribtue is not allowed to be set as multilingual.
When setting any form that has had multilingual enabled as the new key, this field will be automatically reverted to `false`.

isFormGroup.description:
A boolean field indicating whether this form is a form group (if `true`) or a simple form (if `false`).
The default value(`false`) will be hidden from the response.

geographicalRole.description: `None` will be hidden from the response.

timeRole.description: `None` will be hidden from the response.

ms-FormReference.title: Form Reference
ms-FormReference.description:
The reference that identifies a form object within the context of a given attribute.
When writing back an attribute, either `id` or `name` is needed to identify a form, and if both are provided, `id` will take the higher priority.

An array of access control entry objects.  Taken together these objects specify which users are
permitted to perform which actions on this object.

Instead they reuse the access control list of their containing object.

This field is read-only.

String literal used to identify the gross data type of an actual or proposed column in a
database.

scale.description: For relevant data types, the fixed position used in the representation

ms-StructDataType.description: Representation in the object model for a data-type that could be used for a SQL column.

ms-ExpressionModules.title: Fact Expressions
ms-ExpressionModules.description:
Array with a member object for each separately defined expression currently in use by
a fact.  Often a fact expression takes the form of just a single column name, but more complex
expressions are possible.

We say that a table _supports_ a fact if it is possible to compute a value for the fact by
means of a `SELECT` expression for each row of the table.  Each expression represents a
`SELECT` expression.  We have an array of expressions because the architect is not
obligated to use the same expression on every table on which the fact appears.
However it is normal, when a fact appears on multiple tables, for it to reuse an expression,
so we group together tables with the same expression inside this object.

ms-FFSqlPhysicalTable.title: type sql physical table fields
ms-FFSqlPhysicalTable.description: free form sql physical table specific fields

sqlStatement.description: the SQL statement that will be executed against the warehouse to get column data of the physical table.

ms-EncloseSqlInParentheses.title: enclose sql in parentheses
ms-EncloseSqlInParentheses.description:
This property is only applied to free form sql table. By default, this property value is set to true. This encloses the entire SQL that you typed in
the sqlStatement field in parentheses when the SQL statement is executed.

tablePrefix.description: table prefix of this physical table

ms-PhysicalTable.title: physical tables
ms-PhysicalTable.description: Metadata creates a physical table based on warehouse's physical table or free form sql.

ms-logicalSize.description:
MicroStrategy assigns a size to every table in the project when you initially add it to the project.
These size assignments are based on the columns in the tables and the attributes to which those columns correspond.
Because MicroStrategy uses the conceptual or logical attribute definitions to assign a size to each table in the project, this measurement is referred to as logical table size.

ms-isPartOfPartition.description: whether current table is part of partition table.

ms-isTrueKey.description:
MicroStrategy requires each table to have a primary key, which is a unique value identifying each distinct data record or row.
A primary key can be defined by one or more columns in the table.
MicroStrategy determines the primary key for a table based on the attribute's mapped to the columns of the table.
The key is made up of the lowest level attributes.
For example, a warehouse table's primary key is defined using the columns CUSTOMER_ID, PRODUCT_ID, and ORDER_ID.
If these three columns are mapped to attributes in MicroStrategy, then the primary key is represented correctly.
In this case, if isTrueKey is returned as true, it indicates specified primary key is the true key.

ms-PartitionTable.title: Partition Base Table of Metadata Partitioning

partitionLevel.description: An array with references to reduced attributes in the partition filter.

name.description: SAP HANA parameter name that corresponds to SAP HANA variable.

ms-TableDefinition.title: Table definition

tableKey.description: table keys defined on physical table

attributes.description:
Attributes are the business concepts reflected in data.
Attributes provide a context in which to report on and analyze business facts or calculations.
An attribute on a dossier serves as a label for a group of metrics.

facts.description:
Facts are one of the essential elements within the business data model and basis for almost all metrics.
They relate numeric data values from the data warehouse to the MicroStrategy reporting environment.
A fact has two common characteristics: numeric and aggregatable.
Common facts include sales dollars, units sold, profit, and cost.

expression.description:
A fact expression maps facts to physical columns in the warehouse.
These expressions can be as simple as a fact column name from the warehouse, or as sophisticated as a formula containing multiple fact column names and numeric constants.

allOf.description:
Table's data can be from different data sources.
Primary data source is the first option for table's data source.

ms-TableDefinition.description: the definition of a logical table

ms-EnumObjectSubType.title: Object Type
ms-EnumObjectSubType.description:
String literal used to identify the type of a metadata object.

A metadata object has two type related properties known as the _type_ and the _subtype_.
We omit the _type_ from the model-service data model because it is redundant.
The _type_ of an object is implied by its _subtype_.

Other MicroStrategy APIs make a distinction between an object's _type_ and _subtype_ because
in some cases (e.g. the different kinds of reports) it can be useful to see one "report" concept
rather than to have to always distinguish between different kinds of reports.
But there are cases (e.g. *filters* versus *custom-groups*, *users* versus *user-groups*
or *reports* versus *hyper-objects*) where users want to see the different _subtypes_
of the same _type_ as fundamentally different.

ms-ObjectName.title: Metadata Object Name
ms-ObjectName.description:
The name of the metadata object.

It is possible that the metadata might contain translations for the object's name.
In that case the name will be given in the metadata locale of the end user.

An object might not be named at all.  We do not require that all metadata objects are named.
If the object is not named in the metadata then this member value will be omitted.

Each custom group element contains a set of filtering or banding qualifications.  

Qualifications in custom groups are the same as qualifications in filters(Filter REST API), with the following exceptions:
- It has banding qualifications, which are available in custom groups only.
- All set qualifications need to have an output level. A set qualification could be metric or relationship qualification.

Banding qualifications cannot be combined with other types of qualifications

ms-ConsolidationElement.title: Consolidation Element
ms-ConsolidationElement.description:
A consolidation element uses functions to combine attribute elements and consolidation elements into calculations that define a single consolidation element.
Calculation derived elements are created by defining expressions with valid combinations of functions, attribute elements, and derived elements.
You can include the following when you create a consolidation element expression:

- Attribute elements
- Consolidation elements
- Constant
- Functions: You can include `plus`, `minus`, `times`, and `divide`

ms-ExpressionNode.title: Any Node

expressionType.description:
Current used by facts only. The expression type of an expression node.
When writing back a fact object, this value should be acquired from the expressions validate call

dimtyType.description:
Current used by facts only. The dimty type of an expression node.
