ms-ExpressionNode.description:
A single node that is used to build up an expression.
We have a number of different types of expression node, one for each building block that can
appear in an expression.  This schema represents any one of these kinds of nodes.

Some of these nodes (the ones beginning with `predicate...`) are only used within filters and security filter.
Predicates represent part of the filter expression tree that are treated as a single node.

Filter support all kinds of predicates but bandings.
Security Filter support five types of predicates, including PredicateCustomExpression,
PredicateJointElementList, PredicateElementList, PredicateForm, PredicateFilter.
Custom Group supports all kinds of predicates.

ms-ExpressionToken.title: Parser Token

value.description:
The raw text represented by this token.

Generally this text is the same as the text that the user initially typed.
In a few cases the parser may have removed some verbiage.

type.description: Enumeration constant that classifies the text within this token

level.description: Describe the amount of processing performed on this parser token

state.description: Whether token in an error or not

allOf.description: If the token represents an object, provide information about the object

If the token represents an attribute form in the context of an object
(say `City@DESC`) then provide information about the attribute form.

ms-ExpressionToken.description:
Structure that represents a fragment of text that should be handled as one entity when parsing.

The expression parser annotates the text it parses by converting it into an array of _token_ objects.
These objects are used for several purposes:
* They allow us to record extra information (generally the identity of an object) that is not explicitly
  shown in the raw text.  Thus once we have disambiguated a name, it is not necessary to do so again.
* They permit a GUI to reformat the text (using colour, type-face etc.) to make it easier to understand
  the expression.
* They allow the parser to report the location of parse errors within the raw text.

ms-ApplicablePropertyOptions.title: Option object for applicable property

value.description: Option value which can be internationalized.

sqlPreview.description: SQL preview which can be internationalized.

ms-ApplicablePropertyOptions.description: An option object for applicable properties contains a value and a sql preview. The value of each option corresponds to an index in the options array where the option appears.

ms-EnumVariantType.title: Variant Type
ms-EnumVariantType.description: String literal used to identify the type of a variant value

ms-TargetCube.title: Incremental Refresh Report Target Cube
ms-TargetCube.description: A reference to an Intelligence Cube. Executing the Incremental Refresh Report will update this Intelligence Cube.

ms-EnumIncrementType.title: Incremental Refresh Report Increment Type
ms-EnumIncrementType.description:
The increment type specifies the execution way of incremental refresh report:
  - `filter`: use the report filter of the incremental refresh report and target Intelligence Cube's template during execution. The report template of incremental refresh report will be disabled.
  - `report`: use both the template and the filter of incremental refresh report during execution. This option will not work for refreshType `replace`.

ms-EnumRefreshType.title: Incremental Refresh Report Refresh Type
ms-EnumRefreshType.description:
The refresh type specifies how to update target cube:
  - `update`: insert new rows from report data and overwrite overlapping rows between old cube data and report data.
  - `insert`: only insert new, non-overlapping rows from report data.
  - `delete`: remove overlapping rows from old cube data.
  - `update_only`: only overwrite overlapping rows from report data.
  - `replace`: delete rows of old cube data using report filter and replace with report data. Can't be used to update a FFSQL cube.

ms-InnerTemplateForIncrementalRefreshRep.title: Inner Template
ms-InnerTemplateForIncrementalRefreshRep.description:
Object that specifies an _incremental refresh report tempate_. An incremental refresh report template defines a layout of general categories of information to refresh the target cube. In an incremental refresh report template, you specify the information that you want to retrieve form your data source, and the information is used to refresh the target cube.

The units in the Incremental Refresh Report template don't support the types of _custom_group_, _consolidation_, _prompt_ and _raw_unit_.

ms-IncrementalRefreshReportAdvancedPrope.title: Incremental Refresh Report Advanced Properties
ms-IncrementalRefreshReportAdvancedPrope.description: Advanced Properties of the Incremental Refresh Report.

ms-AttributeSortElement.title: Attribute Sort Element
ms-AttributeSortElement.description: An individual sort element in an `AttributeSorts` list.

ms-VLDBProperties.title: VLDB Properties
ms-VLDBProperties.description: VLDB Properties of an object. If we set the VLDBProperty object in the map to be "null", the corresponding property will be set to default.

ms-ObjectAccessControlEntry.title: Access Control Entry

allOf.description: The user or user group whose rights are modified by this entry

rights.description: The access rights granted or denied by this access control entry

isGranted.description: Whether this entry grants (`1`) or denies (`0`) the `rights` to the `trustee`.

ms-ObjectAccessControlEntry.description:
Object that specifies a security restriction that is applied to a single object.

An access control entry specified that a specific _trustee_ (either a _user_ or a _user group_)
is either granted or denied a specific kind of access to this object.

ms-ExpressionModule.title: Fact Expression

tables.description:
Array with a member object for each logical table that computes a value for this fact
by evaluating the expression.

This array should be non-empty for a fact that is saved into metadata,
but may be empty during an edit changeset.
The order of the tables within the array has no significance.

potentialTables.description:
Array with a member object for each logical table that does not currently use
this fact expression, but for which the expression would be valid.

We say that a table is valid for an expression if, for every column name that appears in
the expression, the table has a column with the same name.
It is possible both for this array to be empty and for the content of this array to change
with changing the fact object.
The order of the tables within the array has no significance.

Since this array is relatively expensive to compute (any of the tables in the schema might
support an expression) this array is omitted by default.  A query must be passed in to specify
when a client is interested in saying this array.

ms-ExpressionModule.description:
Object that gives information about an expression that is in use by a fact.
Naturally the most important part of a fact expression is the expression.
This is often just a single column reference, but more complex expressions are supported.
The object also lists those tables where the expression is currently used or where it
could be used.

We say that a table _supports_ a fact if it is possible to compute a value for the fact by
means of a `SELECT` expression for each row of the table.  The expression represents a
`SELECT` expression.

ms-isLogicalSizeLocked.description: Field to indicate whether the logical table size of a table is locked or not. When a table’s logical size is locked, the table is excluded from the logical table size calculation when a schema update is performed.

ms-MDPartitionBaseTableInfoAdd.title: Metadata Partition Base Table Definition

objectId.description: the object ID of an existing logical table

ms-MDPartitionBaseTableInfoAdd.description: body of metadata partition base table

ms-PromptQuestionElements.title: the question part for attribute element prompt
ms-PromptQuestionElements.description: This defines the question for attribute element prompt

listAllElements.description: whether this is list all the elements

ms-PromptQuestionObjects.title: the question part for object prompt
ms-PromptQuestionObjects.description: This defines the question for object prompt

ms-PromptQuestionExpressionMetric.title: the question part for expression metric prompt
ms-PromptQuestionExpressionMetric.description: This defines the question for expression metric prompt

ms-PromptQuestionExpressionAttribute.title: the question part for expression attribute prompt
ms-PromptQuestionExpressionAttribute.description: This defines the question for expression attribute prompt

custForms.description: the cust forms that user selects

ms-PromptQuestionExpressionHierarchy.title: the question part for expression hierarchy prompt
ms-PromptQuestionExpressionHierarchy.description: This defines the question for expression hierarchy prompt

listAllHierarchies.description: whether this is list all the hierarchies

ms-PromptDefaultAnswerValue.title: the default answer part for value prompt
ms-PromptDefaultAnswerValue.description: This defines the default answer for value prompt

value.description: the value for prompt

ms-PromptDefaultAnswerElements.title: the default answer part for attribute element prompt
ms-PromptDefaultAnswerElements.description: This defines the default answer for attribute element prompt

ms-PromptDefaultAnswerObjects.title: the default answer part for object prompt
ms-PromptDefaultAnswerObjects.description: This defines the default answer for object prompt

ms-PromptDefaultAnswerExpression.title: the default answer part for expression prompt
ms-PromptDefaultAnswerExpression.description: This defines the default answer for expression prompt. The default answer is an expression which composed by predicates. For the predicate type, we only support predicate_form_qualification, predicate_element_list, predicate_metric_qualification.

ms-DrillOptions.title: Drill Options

setName.description: A string to help classify the drill path. Drill paths with the same setName will be grouped into the same set

inheritVLDB.description: A flag to control if the new report's VLDB properties are impacted by the source report

keepParent.description: A flag to control whether to keep the selected template unit after drilling

keepThreshold.description: A flag to control whether to keep thresholds in the result report. Only available if the drill path's type is not 'template'

keepPageBy.description: A flag to control whether to keep page-by while drilling to template

keepBaseTemplate.description: A flag to control whether to keep the base template

applyUserFilter.description: A flag to control whether to apply the filter created by the user's selection

applyReportFilter.description: A flag to control whether to apply the original report filter

includeOtherFilter.description:
A flag to control whether to consider other filter qualifications when resolving metric qualifications in the result report. 
Not available if either applyUserFilter or applyReportFilter is set to false

displayMode.description: Default report view mode for the new report after drilling. Only available if the drill path's type is 'template'

ms-DrillOptions.description:
Object that specifies _drill options_.

A Drill Options object is a collection of options that defines the drilling behavior of a drill path

ms-AllOtherElement.title: All other derived element
ms-AllOtherElement.description: All other derived element collects all atrribute elements that are not included in other derived elements, and display them on the report as individual attribute elements by default.

ms-CalculationElement.title: Calculation derived element
ms-CalculationElement.description:
A calculation derived element uses functions to combine attribute elements and derived elements into calculations that define a single derived element.
Calculation derived elements are created by defining expressions with valid combinations of functions, attribute elements, and derived elements.
You can include the following when you create a Calculation derived element expression:

- Attribute elements
- Derived elements
- Constant
- Functions: You can include `abs`, `add`, `average`, `greatest`, `least`, `plus`, `minus`, `times`, and `divide`

ms-FilterElement.title: Filter derived element
ms-FilterElement.description:
A filter derived element uses a filter qualification to determine the combination of attribute elements for a derived element. Filter derived elements are created by filtering
attribute elements to include in each derived element. There are two methods to create Filter derived elements:

1. Create a Filter derived element using a filter qualification on a list of attribute elements. This includes using the In list and Not in List operators.

2. Create a Filter derived element using a filter qualification on attribute forms. You can use various logical and mathematical operators to create filter qualifications
on attribute forms to return data.

allOf.description:
A generic specification for a calculation stored within a metadata object.
The expression is represented as a tree over nodes.  Most internal nodes
(called _operator_ nodes) are defined by applying a function to the operator's child nodes.

An expression must be non-empty. For `tree` nodes, only _Element List Predicate_ and _Attribute Form Predicate_ are supported.

ms-ListElement.title: List derived element
ms-ListElement.description:
A list derived element is also called a group derived element. It is a combination of attribute elements into a single derived element. All attribute element data is added together to
form the metric values for the new list derived element.
List derived elements can only combine attribute elements, they cannot combine other derived elements. If you want to create a derived element that is a combination of other derived
elements, you must use a Calculation derived element.

ms-DimtyUnit.title: DimtyUnit
ms-DimtyUnit.description: Object that specifies the dimty unit.

ms-BaseAttribute.title: baseAttribute
ms-BaseAttribute.description: Object that specifies the base attribute of the transformation attribute.

ms-TransformationAttributeForm.title: transformationAttributeForm
ms-TransformationAttributeForm.description: Object that specifies the transformation attribute form.

ms-DataSourceForNewSubsetReport.title: Data Source for Subset Report
ms-DataSourceForNewSubsetReport.description: The data source of a subset report only contains a cube object reference. The cube need to be published before it is used as data source.

ms-DataSourceForFFSQLReport.title: Data Source for FFSQL Report
ms-DataSourceForFFSQLReport.description:
The dataTemplate definition describes what data you want to include into the report. You are able to add the references of attributes, metrics, prompts, and so on.
The table provides the definition of report's logical table, which includes physicalTable, attributes, metrics and dataSource.

ms-DataSourceForNormalReport.title: Data Source for Normal Report
ms-DataSourceForNormalReport.description:
The dataTemplate definition describes what data you want to include into the report. You are able to add the references of attributes, metrics, prompts, and so on.
By defining a report filter, the data will be filtered accordingly when you retrieve your data from data warehouse.

ms-ViewTemplate.title: Report view template
ms-ViewTemplate.description: Describes the rows and columns presented or hidden in report's grid, as well as how they are sorted (i.e. "sorts" field) and aggregated (i.e. "subtotals" and "pageby" fields).

ms-MetricJoinTypes.title: Metric Join Types
ms-MetricJoinTypes.description: Metric Join Types of a cube/report/IRR.

ms-AttributeJoinTypes.title: Attribute Join Types
ms-AttributeJoinTypes.description: Attribute Join Types of a cube/report/IRR.

ms-DrillOptionsSetting.title: Drill options

value.description: determines whether perform a drill on the report is allowed or not.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: determines whether restrict the drill direction to drill down only. 1 means allow drill everywhere, 2 means only drill down is allowed.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: determines whether the object from which you are drilling is also displayed in the drilled-to report. -1 means yes, 0 means no. 1 means use the drill map's setting.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: determines whether applies the threshold to the drilled-to report. -1 means yes, 0 means no. 1 means use the drill map's setting.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description:
determines whether applies the across-level subtotals to the drilled-to report. -1 means yes, 0 means no. 1 means use the drill map's setting.
Across-level subtotals are hidden only when all of the following are true:
  Inherit across-level subtotal from parent is set to No.
  Keep parent while drilling is set to No.
  You drill from the object that is the level of the subtotal.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: determines whether add any page by field of the report to the filter during drilling

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: 0 means the current page-by element is added to the filter when drilling. 1 means the page-by element is added to the filter when you drill from all the page-by fields of the report.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description: determines whether add any other part of the report to the filter during drilling

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

value.description:
allow you to select what elements are included in the filter when drilling:
  0 means include in the filter only elements of attributes positioned to the left of or above the selected attribute element(s).
  1 means include in the filter all highlighted attribute elements.

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

ms-DrillOptionsSetting.description: Drill options define various settings when performing a drill on the report.

ms-PromptOrdering.title: Prompt Ordering
ms-PromptOrdering.description: A list of prompt information in the order that will be resolved when the report is executed.

ms-DataSourceForSubsetReport.title: Data Source for Subset Report
ms-DataSourceForSubsetReport.description: The data source of a subset report only contains a cube object reference. The cube need to be published before it is used as data source.

ms-TransactionReportPhysicalTable.title: Transaction report physical table
ms-TransactionReportPhysicalTable.description: Metadata creates a physical table based on free-form SQL.

ms-TransactionReportAttribute.title: Transaction Report Attribute

id.description:
Attribute ID

The ID can be:
* the object ID of a managed attribute.
* the object ID of a standalone attribute.

ms-TransactionReportAttribute.description: The Attribute Object Whose Forms Mapped to Transaction Report Columns

ms-TransactionReportMetric.title: Transaction Report Metric
ms-TransactionReportMetric.description: The Metric Object Mapped to a Transaction Report's Column

type.description: The type of this node within the expression tree.

ms-ExpressionNodeOperator.title: Operator Node
ms-ExpressionNodeOperator.description:
An expression node whose value is obtained by applying a specified function to a list of
child expressions.
Most internal nodes within an expression are operator nodes.

children.description:
The list of child expression that combined together at this operator.
If there are multiple child expressions then their order is significant.

In a few cases (depending on the function) it is valid for an operator node to have no
children, in which case this member property may be omitted.

allOf.description:
Enumeration constant that identifies all of the built-in functions.
This value will always be provided when the service generates the object.
When the client generates the object it may use this field by itself to specify the function.
This value by itself is insufficient to identify the function if it is set to `custom` or
`thirdParty`, in which case the `customFunction` value must be provided.

This property is required in the predicate model but can be replaced by 'functionPrompt' in the
expression model.

functionProperties.description:
The list of additional properties associated with the function. This is most useful when the function is 'Rank',
as we'll need the "byValue" and "ascending" properties.

This field is only used in a custom expression tree.

allOf.description:
The prompt used by this function.
This field is only used in a custom expression tree.

levelType.description:
Enumeration value indicating how the predicate's level should be determined.

If there is a level it is usually taken to be the same level as is used in the display grid,
or which was used to obtain the data.  There is also an option to specify an absolute level
as part of the predicate definition.

This field is only used in a custom expression tree.

allOf.description: This field is only used in a custom expression tree.

ms-XdaColumn.title: Column

id.description:
Column ID

The ID can be:
* the object ID used in metadata.
* the object ID used in report instance, but not yet committed to metadata.

ms-XdaColumn.description:
The Column Object definition.
If id is given, model serivce will find the column from physical table, otherwise, name is required to create a new column. Same for XdaAttribute and XdaMetric.

id.description:
Attribute Form ID

The ID can be:
* the object ID used in metadata.
* the object ID used in report instance, but not yet committed to metadata.

Note that Form ID is associated with the form cateogry used. Multiple forms that use the same category will have the same ID.
However, since no two forms within the same attribute can use the same category, no two forms will share an ID in the same attribute.

name.description:
The name of the attribute form set by the attribute.
Unlike `category`, which is the systemic name associated with each reusable form, this name is specific to the attribute using this form.

category.description:
The category of the attribute form.
Unlike `name`, The field is independent of the attribute using this form.
This field can only be set when creating a new form. Once a form is created, its category becomes non-mutable.
If not provided (or set as `None`) when an attribute is being created, a custom category will be automatically generated.

type.description: A read-only field indicating the type of this form. A custom form is created if its category is set to `None`.

ms-DataLanguages.title: Data Languages

items.description: The format should follow the IETF BCP 47 language tag, for example: "en-US".

ms-DataLanguages.description:
The languages setting for the data.
When _project_default_ is selected, the report's data will use the project default data language. The `selectedLanguages` property will be ignored if set.
When _all_project_data_language_ is selected, the report's data will use all project's data language. The `selectedLanguages` property will be ignored if set.
When _specific_languages_ is selected, the report's data will use the languages you specified. The `selectedLanguages` property is required and can't be empty.

ms-EnumDSSCubeRepublishBehaviorType.title: Cube Republish Behavior Type
ms-EnumDSSCubeRepublishBehaviorType.description:
The setting provides different options to refresh Intelligent Cube data.

The _replace_ means that old data will be completely replaced with new data, retrieved by executing the current intelligent cube (using its current template and current filter).

The _dynamic_refresh_ is to insert new rows from new data, and removes non-overlapping rows from old data by comparing the old and new Intelligent Cube data filters.

The _add_ is to insert new rows from new data and overwrites overlapping rows between old and new data.

The _upsert_ is to only insert new non-overlapping rows from new data.

ms-DataPartition.title: Data Partition

allOf.description: The property to speicify the partition attribute. The partition attribute must be in the template.

fetchDataSlicesInParallel.description: Fetch data slices in parallel from the warehouse. You need to disable TPT to enable this function.

ms-DataPartition.description: Object specifies the data partition behavior of the cube. You can select a data partition key (attribute) and the number of partitions to publish data in-memory.

ms-BandingDistinct.title: Banding distinct predicate
ms-BandingDistinct.description:
The Band for each distinct metric value type of banding qualification creates a separate band for each value calculated by the metric. 
The bands appear as rows on a report. This type of banding qualification directly uses the results of a metric as bands.

It is very useful with metrics that already contain the logic needed to calculate sequential band numbers. 
Such metrics use mathematical formulas, NTile functions, Band functions, or Case functions.

ms-BandingPoints.title: Banding points predicate
ms-BandingPoints.description:
The Banding points type of banding qualification slices a range of metric values into a number of equal bands that appear as rows on a report. 

You manually define each band, which allows you to produce bands of varying sizes.

ms-BandingCount.title: Banding count predicate
ms-BandingCount.description:
The Band count type of banding qualification slices a range of metric values into a number of equal bands that appear as rows on a report. 

You define the range by setting the start at and stop at values. You also set the band count, which is the number of bands to use.

ms-BandingSize.title: Banding size predicate
ms-BandingSize.description:
The Band size type of banding qualification slices a range of metric values into a number of bands that appear as rows on a report. 

You define the range by setting the start at and stop at values. You also set the step size, which is the size of each band.

children.description:
Array containing the optional root node of the child filter expression.
We require that a relationship predicate always has a child filter is always given, but it is possible that the
child filter is the _empty filter_.  We model that situation by either omitting the `children` value
or by using an empty array.

This expression is also a filter expression, and it is manipulated in the same manner
as the rest of the filter.
The predicate ids that appear within this expression must have disjoint values from the
predicate ids used in the larger filter.  Thus an individual node within this expression
can be manipulated by treating it as a manipulation of the larger filter in the usual
manner.

We use an array so that this value matches the array value of the same name used in an operator node.

dataLocale.description:
Optional data locale used to select values of the form.
The format should follow the IETF BCP 47 language tag, for example: "en-US".

Some forms of some attributes may be configured to store representation in multiple
locales.  If a predicate qualifies on a form that has been translated in this manner
it is likely that the elements selected by the predicate will be different for
different locales.  But we do not want the meaning of a filter to vary based on the
locale preferences of the user executing the filter.  So this property is used to
specify the data locale to be used for the qualification.  When set it will override
the user's personal data locale preference.

ms-ExpressionNodeConstant.title: Constant Node
ms-ExpressionNodeConstant.description:
An expression node that contains a literal value.  The value was fixed when the expression
node was first specified.  This kind of node is only used for simple values (integers, floating
point numbers and text strings).

Alternatively, instead of the variant the node could contain a choose-value prompt reference.

ms-ExpressionNodeColumnReference.title: Column Reference Node
ms-ExpressionNodeColumnReference.description:
An expression node whose value is some column from a table implied by the expression's context.

This kind of node is used when we wish to define an expression over the columns of a table or
potentially over more than one table.
The reference specifies the column of interest by name alone.  So the reference is to a selected
column on the implied table.  It is not a reference to a specific column on a specific table.
Defining a column reference in this manner allows the architect to specify an expression that
can be usefully applied to more than one table.

columnName.description: The name of the column, as it appears within the RDBMS.

ms-ExpressionNodeObjectReference.title: Object Reference Node
ms-ExpressionNodeObjectReference.description:
An expression node whose value is a reference to some other object in the metadata.

The meaning of the reference depends on the context.
If the referenced object is an object that is based on an expression (say a filter or a metric)
then it usually means we inline the expression from the object.  But in other cases (say a fact)
it means that on execution the object reference is replaced with a value of the fact in the
context of execution.

Although unusual, it is possible that an object reference node might not refer to an object.
If that happens this member property is usually omitted (but might be present but use
`00000000000000000000000000000000` as its `objectId`).

The object could be a prompt to select the target object at runtime.
Typically it is not acceptable for the prompt to return more than one answer.

allOf.description: optionally this target can be replaced by a prompt object.

isIndependent.description:
Flag that indicates whether this child filter will be considered independently of other parts
of the larger filter:
* If set to `1` (the default value, used if this property is omitted) then this filter will
  be evaluated by itself.
* If set to `0` then other parts of the larger filter will be merged into this filter.
  Using this setting could change the value of a _metric_ or _relationship_ set qualification
  that appears within the child filter.

substituteFunctionType.description:
Specify how to merge multiple answers if the predicate's value is obtained via a prompt.

This property is only used when _filter_'s value is a prompt.
If the end user selects multiple answers to the prompt, the platform will replace this node
by a Boolean operator, used to combine the answers.  This property determines which operator
it uses.  By default it uses the parent Boolean operator (or _AND_ if this predicate is
the root of the filter).  Set this property to override the default behavior.

ms-PredicatePrompt.title: Prompt Predicate
ms-PredicatePrompt.description:
Specialized expression node for a predicate whose value is prompted at execution time.

This predicate is used for various kinds of expression prompts.
At execution time the end user in effect enters in an entire predicate (or an even more
complex expression).  The answer is then substituted into the filter in place of this node.

ms-PredicateReport.title: Report Qualification Predicate
ms-PredicateReport.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a report qualification predicate.

This node is used within filter expressions to represent a predicate whose value is
determined by using a _report as filter_.  That is the engine will evaluate a report, and determine
the rows that it covers.  These filter will consist of these rows.

allOf.description:
Reference to the child report that should be included in this predicate.

Alternatively the filter designer might choose to postpone the decision of which
report to include until runtime.  The designer would do this by referencing a choose-objects
prompt instead of the report.  The prompt must be configured to return zero or more
report objects.  If the designer selects a prompt that is answered with multiple
reports then the optional `substituteFunctionType` is used to determine how to
combine these reports together.

ms-PredicateFilter.title: Filter Qualification Predicate
ms-PredicateFilter.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a filter qualification predicate.

This node is used within filter expressions to represent a predicate whose value is
determined by using an external _filter_ object.  At execution time the engine will
typically act as if the specified filter had been included directly.
By using shared filter objects, the client can build complex filter expressions
the utilise common subexpressions.

A filter object may not reference itself, or any filter that depends on itself.
If such a filter is saved to the metadata any calculation that uses the filter
will fail.

allOf.description:
Reference to the child filter that should be included in this predicate.

Alternatively the filter designer might choose to postpone the decision of which
filter to include to runtime.  The designer would do this by referencing a choose-objects
prompt instead of the filter.  The prompt must be configured to return zero or more
filter objects.  If the designer selects a prompt that is answered with multiple
filters then the optional `substituteFunctionType` is used to determine how to
combine these filters together.

isIndependent.description:
Flag that indicates whether this child filter will be considered independently of other parts
of the larger filter:
* If set to `1` (the default value, used if this property is omitted) then this filter will
  be evaluated by itself.
* If set to `0` then other parts of the larger filter will be merged into this filter.
  Using this setting could change the value of a _metric_ or _relationship_ set qualification
  that appears within the child filter.

ms-PredicateForm.title: Attribute Form Predicate
ms-PredicateForm.description:
Specialized expression node that contains an attribute form qualification predicate.

This qualification selects elements of a specified attribute by comparing the value
of a specified form of the element with an expression.
The comparison operator used is also specified.  However there are three options for
specifying the expression with which it is compared:
* The client may specify a literal value.
* The client may ask to use a prompt to determine the value.
* The client may supply an arbitrary metric style expression to evaluate to obtain the value.

allOf.description: The attribute whose elements are selected by this qualification
The attribute form whose value is used to specify the qualification

dataLocale.description:
Optional data locale used to select values of the form.
The format should follow the IETF BCP 47 language tag, for example: "en-US".

Some forms of some attributes may be configured to store representation in multiple
locales.  If a predicate qualifies on a form that has been translated in this manner
it is likely that the elements selected by the predicate will be different for
different locales.  But we do not want the meaning of a filter to vary based on the
locale preferences of the user executing the filter.  So this property is used to
specify the data locale to be used for the qualification.  When set it will override
the user's personal data locale preference.

When the attribute form is set to multilingual,
the dataLocale will take effect when displaying the data in the report;
if not, the dataLocale setting will be ignored.
However, you can always set the dataLocale regardless of whether the form is multilingual or not,
as the form's multilingual setting could be changed.
If no particular locale is set, an empty string will be returned.
If the field is omitted from the input, the locale will default
to the warehouse data locale of the user.

ms-PredicateElementList.title: Element List Predicate
ms-PredicateElementList.description:
Specialized expression node that contains an element list predicate.

This qualification selects elements of a specified attribute by either listing the elements
that should be selected, or alternatively by listing the elements that should be
excluded from the selection.

allOf.description:
The attribute whose elements are selected by this qualification.
Property is not needed if the predicate uses a prompt since in that
case the attribute is specified by the prompt.

elements.description: Array listing the elements in the predicate

allOf.description:
A choose-elements prompt used to obtain the elements in the qualification.
If this object is used then it will specify the attribute and elements
for the qualification.  The prompt will specify an attribute from which
the elements should be selected.

Specify the function that should be used in this predicate.

Although this property is a function identifier, there are only two functions that
make sense for this predicate:
* `in` (the filter accepts any element in the list, default value)
* `notIn` (the filter accepts any element *not* included in the list)

ms-PredicateJointElementList.title: Joint Element List Predicate
ms-PredicateJointElementList.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a joint element list predicate.

This qualification represents a filter at the level of two or more attributes.
The filter is defined by simply listing the tuples of elements that satisfy
the filter.

level.description: The level of this filter represented as an array of attributes. The attributes must be different from each other, and their order is significant.

tuples.description:
An array of tuples of elements.

Each element of this array is a _joint element_.
That is the members of this array are themselves arrays of elements.
The elements in the tuples *must* be listed in the same order as the attributes were
listed in the `level` array.  There is no need to list the attribute on each element.

items.description: A tuple of elements, with one element for each attribute in the `level`

ms-PredicateRelationship.title: Set from Relationship Predicate
ms-PredicateRelationship.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a relationship set qualification predicate.
In words this predicate describes a qualification of the form
"SET of <level> WHERE <an arbitrary filter is true>".

This node is used within filter expressions to represent a predicate whose value is
determined by starting with an arbitrary filter expression at some natural level.
This filter is then projected to a filter at a level specified within this predicate
by using a relationship.  The relationship might be the natural relationship inferred
by the engine for the schema, the relationship given by an explicit table, or a relationship
implied by a fact (i.e. the engine selects the best table from the set of tables where the
given fact is implemented).

This predicate is the only kind of filter predicate that may have a child expression.
Note that the child expression (if we have any) is *not* listed under the `predicateTree` value
but instead appear using the same array value as is used to place the children under an operator node.
This indicates that the child expression of a relationship predicate is shown even if the caller
did not ask to see predicate details.

children.description:
Array containing the optional root node of the child filter expression.
We require that a relationship predicate always has a child filter is always given, but it is possible that the
child filter is the _empty filter_.  We model that situation by either omitting the `children` value
or by using an empty array.

This expression is also a filter expression, and it is manipulated in the same manner
as the rest of the filter.
The predicate ids that appear within this expression must have disjoint values from the
predicate ids used in the larger filter.  Thus an individual node within this expression
can be manipulated by treating it as a manipulation of the larger filter in the usual
manner.

We use an array so that this value matches the array value of the same name used in an operator node.

ms-PredicateMetric.title: Metric Predicate
ms-PredicateMetric.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a metric qualification predicate.

This qualification selects tuples of elements at a specified level by comparing the value
of a specified metric evaluated at the tuple with a value of an expression.
The comparison operator used is also specified.  However there are multiple options for
specifying the expression with which the metric is compared:
* The client may specify a literal value.
* The client may specify an object reference.
* The client may ask to use a prompt to determine the value.
* The client may supply an arbitrary metric style expression to evaluate to obtain the value.

levelType.description:
Enumeration value indicating how the predicate's level should be determined.

If there is a level it is usually taken to be the same level as is used in the display grid,
or which was used to obtain the data.  There is also an option to specify an absolute level
as part of the predicate definition.

allOf.description:
The metric over whose values this qualification is evaluated.

Alternatively the filter designer could elect to leave the selection of the metric
to the end user by using a prompt reference.  If that's done then the prompt
reference should be to a prompt that is configured to return at most one metric
object.  If the end user does not select a metric at run time then this
predicate will be ignored.

metricFunction.description:
Optional parameter that describes how the metric is used within the predicate.
If this parameter is omitted it is treated as if it had been set to `value`.
* `value`: The qualification is applied directly to the metric's values.
* `rankDescend`: The values taken by the metric over the tuples within the domain of the filter
  are ranked in descending order.  The metric is replaced by its position (one-based) within
  this ranking.
  If the metric's value is identical for two tuples then they are given the same ranking
  (and a gap is left afterwards).  So if the metric values were [23.5, 7.1, 23.5, 6.9] then
  the ranked values would be [1, 3, 1, 4].
  This function can be used to obtain a filter that ranges over the top 10 values that
  the metric achieves.
* `rankAscend`: This is identical to `rankDescend` except that this time the metric's values
  are ascending.  Thus this metric function is used to find the bottom 10 values (say) for
  the metric.
* `percentileDescend`: The values are ranked in descending order.  The metric is then assigned
  a floating point value corresponding to its percentile position within the entire range.
  Thus the median value would be taken as `50` within the comparison operator.
  As usual if two tuples have the same metric value then they will be given the same percentile
  value as well.
* `percentileAscend`: This is identical to `percentileDescend` except that the metric values are
  ranked in ascending order instead.

nullInclude.description:
NullInclude is an integer parameter which specifies how null values are considered when evaluating the Rank function. Use `1` to place null values after the value list, use `-1` to place null values before the value list or use `0` to apply the Null checking setting for Analytical Engine in VLDB properties.
If this value is not set, `0` will be applied.

allOf.description:
The break by property of a metric qualification allows you to choose the attribute level
at which to restart counting rank or percent values for a metric.
This attribute level must be higher than or equal to the level of aggregation for the metric itself.
BreakBy is a list of distinct attribute references.
The array may also contain a choose-attribute prompt instead which can be answered with
zero, one or more attributes.

This value is only used when the `metricFunction` property is not set to `value`.

isIndependent.description:
Flag that indicates whether the metric will be evaluated independently of other parts
of the larger filter:
* If set to `1` (the default value, also used if this property is omitted) then this metric will
  be evaluated by itself.
* If set to `0` then other parts of the larger filter will be applied when evaluating the metric.

ms-PredicateCustomExpression.title: Custom Expression Predicate
ms-PredicateCustomExpression.description:
Specialized expression node that contains a custom expression predicate.

This qualification contains a valid expression, usually created by the user, which can't be categorized into
other types.

The expression is represented by an array of tokens. The predicate has the predicate details which contains the tokens
representing the expression. It's controlled by the `showPredicates` to show or hide.

ms-VLDBProperty.title: VLDB Property

value.description: The Lean variant value

resolvedLocation.description: The location where the property value is resolved

nextValue.description: The Lean variant next value, will be returned only if isInherited is false

nextResolvedLocation.description: The next location where the property value is resolved, will be returned only if isInherited is false

ms-VLDBProperty.description: VLDB Property of an object. If we set the value to be "null", the VLDBProperty will be set to default.

ms-EnumAccessRight.title: Access Right Type
ms-EnumAccessRight.description: String literal used to identify a single access right

ms-StructColumn.title: Column
ms-StructColumn.description:
Object that represents a physical column that might appear in some data source.
In addition to representing physical columns, we also use this object to represent columns
that do not actually appear in any data source but which the engine should create if it needs
to make a column to contain data for some higher level construct
(e.g. a _fact_, an _attribute form_ etc.).

A column consists of a name used to identify the column in its context (a table)
and its data type.

ms-PartitionFilterExpression.title: Partition Filter Expression

text.description:
Human readable description of the expression.  It is generated from the current specification
of the expression.

This string will appear similar to a parsable description of the expression in
the current user's locale.  It is intended to be used to allow a user to easily
distinguish between expressions.  But this string cannot actually be used as input to a
parser session because it does not contain hidden information about ambiguities in
the parse text.

Since this representation is not able to fully describe an expression, there is
no point in the client ever sending it to the service.

tokens.description:
Optional array, used if the expression is to be presented as a stream of tokens.

All types of expressions can be represented in the form of tokens, but some expressions with type predicate are yet not supported to be used in the request input for validation or modification, even when you are using the tokens returned from the service. For e.g. predicate_joint_element_list,  predicate_element_list, and some cases involving embedded objects. Expected errors while be thrown in such cases.

When this representation is used by the service we would expect that the tokens would either
completely parse the expression, or would describe an error in the parsing.  When this representation
is used by the client the tokens would either correspond to fresh text from the user (a `client` token)
or to parts of the text that the user has not edited (a `parsed` token).

ms-PartitionFilterExpression.description:
A generic specification for a calculation stored within a metadata object.
The expression is represented as a tree over nodes.  Most internal nodes
(called _operator_ nodes) are defined by applying a function to the operator's child nodes.

Usually an expression must be non-empty.  But in a few cases, most notably a filter expression
it is valid for an expression to contain no nodes at all.  An expression is _empty_ if and only if
it does not have a `tree` property.

Alternatively the client may prefer to handle an expression as a list of tokens.
Each token represents part of the raw text of the expression, in some cases annotated
with additional information.

ms-ElementConcrete.title: Attribute Element

display.description: Human readable string to display to describe the element to a reader

elementId.description:
Opaque string value that identifies the element to the service.

The client should make no assumption about the format of this string, and should not attempt to
parse it (even though forms of the element are likely to be visibly concatenated into it).
This string will remain valid even if the current client is using a different locale to the
locale of the client that created the element.

When creating an element, the client should copy this string from other fields of the
element browse result.  If a "terse" element id is used, the service will need to know the
attribute in order to decode the element id.

allOf.description:
Optionally the element may specify the attribute that it uses.

Note that in most cases elements are used in a context in which the attribute value
is implied (for example the element is part of an IN-list of elements for some attribute).
if the attribute is implied there is no need to redundantly repeat it on each element
in the collection.

ms-ElementConcrete.description:
This object represents a concrete element.
A concrete element is a metadata representation of an element that exists in some data source.

Since an external data source can be modified without using the MicroStrategy Platform, the
existence of a concrete element in the metadata does not mean that the element described
currently exists.  It means that it existed at some point.

It is possible (using the feature known as _data internationalization_) that a data source
may provide multiple descriptions of the same element in different locales.  But all interactions
with a data source are performed using a single locale.  It would be highly inefficient to
retrieve all translations of all elements in case the end user might later decide to place
the element into the metadata.  The upshot of this paragraph is that concrete elements are
mono-lingual in the metadata.

type.description: type of the derived element element

format.description:
Format that lets you highlight important or unique data as well as correctly reflect value and character formats for your derived elements. _header_ allow you to format
individual attribute elements, while _value_ allow you to format metric values associated with the individual attribute elements.

options.description:
For options field, it is the same for list, filter, calculation, All Other element.

"display" controls the element behavior. it has two values: "this_elements" means consolidate items into one element. "individual_items" means keep individual items separate.

"subtotal" controls the subtotal behavior, it has three values. It is similar to the "display" field, except it controls subtotal instead of element when applied in document.

"includeIndividual" enables analyzing attribute elements with derived elements, while also including the attribute element on the report. It only affects list element, filter element and calculation element.

ms-DataTemplate.title: Report Data template
ms-DataTemplate.description:
For normal report, attribute, custom group, consolidation, object prompt, dimension and metrics can be added to data template units. Drill map can be set to data template as well.

For free form SQL report, the data template is generated according to its table. If dataTemplate is given in request body, only derived element in attribute template unit will be updated. Drill map cannot be set to data template.

Subset report does not have data template.

ms-TemplateAxis.title: Report view template axis
ms-TemplateAxis.description: Describes the objects shown in grid.

ms-DrillMapReference.title: Drill Map reference
ms-DrillMapReference.description: A reference to a drill map object

nextNoParticipationInPreserveRow.description: Next no participation in preserve row, will be returned only if isInherited is false

ms-EnumDSSPropertyLocation.title: Property Location
ms-EnumDSSPropertyLocation.description: String literal used to identify the location where the property value is resolved

ms-isInherited.description: Whether the current property value is inherited from other objects or not. If the property is set on the object, the isInherited is false. If the property's value is inherited from other objects, such as project, dbrole or other objects in the look up chain, the isInherited is true.

required.description: whether this prompt is required to answer during report execution

sortable.description: whether this prompt order can be changed or not

ms-TransactionReportColumn.title: Transaction Report Column
ms-TransactionReportColumn.description:
The Column Object definition.
In the request body of POST transaction report API, you should not provide the ID of the column.
In the request body of PUT transaction report API, if ID is given, model serivce will find the column from physical table, otherwise, name is required to create a new column.

ms-TransactionReportAttributeForm.title: Transaction Report Attribute Form

name.description:
The name of the attribute form set by the attribute.
Unlike `category`, which is the systemic name associated with each reusable form, this name is specific to the attribute using this form.

category.description:
The category of the attribute form.
Unlike `name`, The field is independent of the attribute using this form.
This field can only be set when creating a new form. Once a form is created, its category becomes non-mutable.
If not provided (or set as `None`) when an attribute is being created, a custom category will be automatically generated.

type.description: A read-only field indicating the type of this form. A custom form is created if its category is set to `None`.

ms-TransactionReportAttributeForm.description: The Attribute Form Mapped to a Transaction Report's Column

alias.description: The alias of the object in the template unit

ms-MetricsTemplateUnit.title: Metrics Template Unit
ms-MetricsTemplateUnit.description: A template unit referes to a list of metrics.

ms-AttributeTemplateUnit.title: Attribute Template Unit
ms-AttributeTemplateUnit.description:
A template unit refers to an attribute. When the `forms` is _null_, the attribute will use the `displayForms` of the specific attribute. When the `forms` is not null, the report using the template unit will use the forms defined instead of the default value.

The unit has to point to an attribute and duplicate forms in the unit are not allowed.

ms-EnumFunction.title: Function Identifier
ms-EnumFunction.description:
String literal used to identify built-in function objects

In nearly all cases when a client wishes to refer to a function, the client will want to refer
to one of the built-in functions.  This enumeration provides values for these functions.
A value from this enumeration may be used instead of an explicit object reference when
referring to one of these functions.

ms-FunctionProperty.title: Function Property
ms-FunctionProperty.description: additional property associated with a function.

ms-NodeLevel.title: Node Level
ms-NodeLevel.description:
Within an expression we sometimes need to state at which a computation is performed.
In some cases the level is obtained by examining the execution environment.
This object is used if the designer wishes to specify an explicit level.

An explicit level is a list of attributes.  Alternatively the client may defer
selecting an attribute (or attributes) at design time by using a prompt instead.
If a prompt is used it should be a choose-attributes prompt, and it may return
any number of attributes (including zero).

ms-NodeProperty.title: Node Property
ms-NodeProperty.description:
A property used for persisting back-compatibility information for the filter editing. Usually it will not show. If it's displayed in the
response, you should consider include it with the same value in the same node in the request body when you performing the PUT/POST/PATCH.

If you don't put it back in the request, there can be some behavior difference in Filter Expression Editors of other MicroStrategy apps.

ms-ExpressionNodeBase.title: Expression Node (Base)

type.description: The type of this node within the expression tree

ms-ExpressionNodeBase.description: Common properties that are shared by all the kinds of Expression node

bandNames.description: array of band names, a band name cannot contain "#;" or "#,"

ms-PredicateBase.title: Predicate Node (Base)
ms-PredicateBase.description:
Base schema for all expression nodes that are considered to be predicates.

A filter predicate is a node that represents a single qualification in a filter expression.
Predicate nodes have a couple of extra shared values, which are included using
this schema.  All of the predicate node's predicate specific properties are placed under a single
object with key `predicateTree`.

predicateId.description:
String identifier that uniquely identifies this predicate within the context of its larger filter.
Any non-empty string of printable characters can be used as a predicate.

These identifiers have no runtime significance.  They are used so we can apply manipulations to
a specific predicate within a filter.  The id of a specific predicate will not change as the
predicate is moved around during an edit changeset.  On the other hand predicate ids are not
saved into metadata.  Thus the client should expect that different ids are used for each
edit changeset.

TODO: Can the client assign a predicate id, or is that a prerogative of the service?

ms-StructVariant.title: Variant

value.description: The value, encoded as a string in a standard, locale-neutral, format.

ms-StructVariant.description:
A typed value.  It is usually an integer or a string.

The value is represented as a string in a standard format.
A value type must also be provided to specify the actual data type of the value.

ms-RelationshipShared.title: Shared components in a relationship qualification

allOf.description:
An optional object that specifies how to relate the child filter with the output filter.
There are three options, depending on the nature of the `guide` object:
* The object may be a `logicalTable`, in which case the filter is projected through this table.
* The object may be a `fact`, in which case the filter is projected via one of the tables on
  which the fact is defined.
* There might not be a guide object at all, in which case the filter is projected via the
  engine's usual join rules (using attribute relationships from the system hierarchy).

isIndependent.description:
Flag that indicates whether the child filter will be considered independently of other parts
of the larger filter:
* If set to `1` (the default value, also used if this property is omitted) then this filter will
  be evaluated by itself.
* If set to `0` then other parts of the larger filter will be merged into this filter.

ms-StructDynamicDateTime.title: Dynamic Date/Time Structure
ms-StructDynamicDateTime.description:
Object that represents a date/time. It can be date-only, or time-only, or date-time.
The date part and the time part can be dynamic or static independently. Typically 
this object is used when at least one of the two parts is dynamic. Although it's
capable of describing a static date/time, the much simpler Variant is preferable 
in that case.

allOf.description: The date component, required when the type is date or date_time
The time component, required when the type is time or date_time

ms-PredicateExpressionShared.title: Predicate Expression Shared

allOf.description:
Specify the comparison function that should be used to compare the value computed by
the filter with an expression value.

Usually we would expect one of the following functions but other functions are permitted:
* `equals`
* `not_equal`
* `greater`
* `greater_equal`
* `less`
* `less_equal`
* `is_null` (takes no additional arguments)
* `is_not_null` (takes no additional arguments)
* `begins_with` (string values only)
* `not_begins_with` (string values only)
* `ends_with` (string values only)
* `not_ends_with` (string values only)
* `contains` (string values only)
* `not_contains` (string values only)
* `like` (string values only)
* `not_like` (string values only)
* `in` (argument must be array of literals)
* `not_in` (argument must be array of literals)
* `between` (takes two arguments)
* `not_between` (takes two arguments)

For a complete list of supported functions, please refer to the EnumFunction.

parameters.description:
Array of objects that correspond to the parameters for the comparison function.
In nearly all cases the comparison function will take one extra parameter (in addition to the parameter
referenced by the predicate's main object).

This value is an array because there are a few functions which take a different number of extra parameters.
For example `isNull` does not need any additional parameters; `notBetween` needs two additional parameters.

ms-PredicateExpressionShared.description: Miscellaneous values shared between several schema (not interesting by themselves)

ms-PartitionFilterExpressionPredicateNod.title: Partition Filter Predicate Node
ms-PartitionFilterExpressionPredicateNod.description: A single predicate node that is used to build a partition filter expression.

alias.description: The alias of the template metric element

thresholds.description: The list of threshold defined for the metric. Thresholds can only be defined on view template.

allOf.description:
Metric limit expression. The object used in metric limit must exist in report template.

The metric limit can only be defined on view template.

ms-EvaluationOrder.title: Evaluation Order
ms-EvaluationOrder.description: Evaluation order controls the order in which compound smart metrics, consolidations, derived metrics, derived elements, report limits, and subtotals are calculated and resolved for a given report.

ms-TemplateSubtotalInfoReference.title: Subtotal Object Info Reference
ms-TemplateSubtotalInfoReference.description: the subtotal object info reference used in template subtotal

ms-TemplateMetricSubtotals.title: Template Metric Subtotals
ms-TemplateMetricSubtotals.description: the custom defined metric subtotal list used in `subtotal_list` template subtotal

type.description: String literal used to identify the object type, according to values from EnumDSSObjectType.

alias.description: The alias of the template metric element

ms-StructVersatileTime.title: Versatile Time

hour.description: The hour component (0-23). Used when the hour is static.

hourOffset.description: The offset from the current hour. Used when the hour is dynamic.

minute.description: The minute component (0-59). Used when the minute is static.

minuteOffset.description: The offset from the current minute. Used when the minute is dynamic.

second.description: The second component (0-59). Used when the second is static.

secondOffset.description: The offset from the current second. Used when the second is dynamic.

ms-StructVersatileTime.description: Object that represents either a dynamic time or a static time

ms-StructVersatileDate.title: Versatile Date
ms-StructVersatileDate.description: Object that represents either a dynamic date or a static date.

ms-PredicateParameter.title: Any Predicate Parameter
ms-PredicateParameter.description: One of the various mechanisms used to specify a predicate parameter

type.description: A unit describes the layout of the grid for the report.

ms-MetricHierarchicalSort.title: Metric Hierarchical Sort
ms-MetricHierarchicalSort.description: the metric sort with hierarchical flag defined

ms-CustomGroupSort.title: Custom Group Sort
ms-CustomGroupSort.description: the sort defined on custom group

ms-AttributeFormSort.title: Attribute Form Sort
ms-AttributeFormSort.description: the sort defined on attribute form

ms-TemplateMetricInfoReference.title: Metric Info Reference

isEmbedded.description:
If true indicates that the target object of this reference is embedded within this object.
Alternatively if this object is itself embedded, then it means that the target object is
embedded in the same container as this object.

If this field is omitted (as is usual) then the target is not embedded.

ms-TemplateMetricInfoReference.description: the metric object info reference used in template

ms-StructVersatileDateStatic.title: Versatile Date (static)

value.description: ISO 8601 string of the date component

ms-StructVersatileDateStatic.description: Object that represents a static date

ms-StructVersatileDateDynamic.title: Versatile Date (dynamic)

excludeWeekends.description: Whether weekend days should be excluded during calculation

ms-StructVersatileDateDynamic.description:
Object that represents a dynamic date. Its various settings define a function that will
resolve to a date using the current date as the input.

adjustmentNone.description: Indicate that there is no further adjustments

adjustmentWeeklyByDayOfWeek.description: Adjustment at the week level by day of week

adjustmentMonthlyByDay.description: Adjustment at the month level by day

adjustmentMonthlyByDayOfWeek.description: Adjustment at the month level by day of week

days.description: Number of days to reverse count

months.description: Number of months to reverse count

adjustmentMonthlyByReverseCount.description: Adjustment at the month level by reverse count

adjustmentQuarterlyByDay.description: Adjustment at the quarter level by day

monthOrdinal.description: Number indicating 1st, 2nd, or 3rd month in the quarter

adjustmentQuarterlyByDayOfWeek.description: Adjustment at the quarter level by day of week

days.description: Number of days to reverse count

adjustmentQuarterlyByReverseCount.description: Adjustment at the quarter level by reverse count

adjustmentYearlyByDate.description: Adjustment at the year level by date

adjustmentYearlyByDayOfWeek.description: Adjustment at the year level by day of week

ms-PredicateParameterDynamicDateTime.title: Dynamic Date/Time Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterDynamicDateTime.description:
Object used to describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value
is a dynamic date/time.

ms-PredicateParameterArray.title: Constant Array Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterArray.description:
Object used to describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value is a list
of constants.

This kind of parameter is very specific.  It is used for the `in` and `notIn` comparison functions.
These functions compare one value with an array of constant values.
Note that all of the values in the array must have the same type.

constants.description:
Array of constant values, each of which is encoded as a string in a standard, locale-neutral
representation.  Their common datatype is given by `constantsType`.

ms-PredicateParameterPrompt.title: Prompt Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterPrompt.description:
Object used to describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value will
be obtained on execution by resolving a _prompt object_.

The prompt could be any type of prompt that returns a simple value: integer prompt, double prompt,
big-decimal prompt, string prompt or date prompt.
The prompt should be consistent with the data type of the value to be compared with the prompt.

ms-PredicateParameterExpression.title: Expression Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterExpression.description:
Object used to describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value is
given as a custom expression.
This predicate parameter option is used for when the end user wants to provide a custom
expression for the predicate.  We would expect the parser to be used to modify the expression.

ms-PredicateParameterObject.title: Object Reference Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterObject.description:
Object that describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value is an object reference.
For example, a parameter of this type would be used by a filter predicate that compares the value of two
metrics.  This would be modelled by using a metric predicate (containing one of the two metrics);
this parameter would then be used to record the other metric.

allOf.description: Reference to the object whose value is compared with the object in the predicate.

ms-PredicateParameterConstant.title: Constant Parameter in Predicate
ms-PredicateParameterConstant.description: Object used to describe a parameter that appears in a filter predicate and whose value is constant.

allOf.description: The typed value used in the predicate

alias.description: The alias of the object in the template unit

ms-ReportMetricsTemplateUnit.title: Metrics Template Unit
ms-ReportMetricsTemplateUnit.description: A template unit refers to a list of metrics.

allOf.description:
`predicate_metric_qualification`, `predicate_prompt_qualification` and `operator` nodes are supported.
 Only normal report has report limit. Free form SQL report and subset report does not have report limit.

ms-ReportAttributeTemplateUnit.title: Attribute Template Unit
ms-ReportAttributeTemplateUnit.description:
A template unit refers to an attribute. When the `forms` is _null_, the attribute will use the `displayForms` of the specific attribute. When the `forms` is not null, the report using the template unit will use the forms defined instead of the default value.

The unit has to point to an attribute and duplicate forms in the unit are not allowed.

`limit` represents that there is a derived element defined on this attribute.

allOf.description: An expression maps attribute template unit to a derived element. `type` must be _reference_.

ms-SubtotalsPosition.title: Subtotals Position
ms-SubtotalsPosition.description:
Subtotals position is used to define where the subtotals is after apply current sort.
It support following values:
  - `mixed`
  - `top`
  - `bottom`
  - `inherit`

ms-SortOrder.title: Sort Order
ms-SortOrder.description: the sort order used in template sort

ms-SortOnElements.title: Sort On Element List
ms-SortOnElements.description:
Sort on element list is used in MetricSort or MetricHierarchicalSort. We can define sort on following elements:
 - `concreteElement`: concrete element in attribute
 - `consolidationElement`: cosolidation element in consolidation
 - `customGroupElement`: custom group element in custom group
 - `subtotalElement`: subtotal element in template subtotals

ms-SubtotalReferenceInSort.description: the subtotal reference used in sort definition

ms-TemplateCustomGroupInfoReference.title: Custom Group Object Info Reference

ms-CustomGroupSortType.title: Custom Group Sort Type
ms-CustomGroupSortType.description:
Custom group sort type is used to define which criteria current sort is applied on.
It support following values:

ms-ParentFirst.title: Parent First

ms-ShowAdvancedPropertiesForCube.name: showAdvancedProperties
ms-ShowAdvancedPropertiesForCube.description:
Specify whether to retrieve the values of the advanced properties.

The advanced properties are presented in the following groups:
* "vldbProperties": A list of properties as determined by the common infrastructure.
* "metricJoinTypes": A list of Metric Join Types, one for each metric that appears in the template.
* "attributeJoinTypes": A list of Attribute Join Types, one for each attribute that appears in the template.

If omitted or `false`, nothing will be returned for the advanced properties.

If `true`, all applicable advanced properties are returned.

ms-ShowFilterTokensForCube.name: showFilterTokens
ms-ShowFilterTokensForCube.description:
Specify whether the cube "filter" is returned in "tokens" format, along with "text" and "tree" formats.

The cube filter is presented in the following formats:
* "text": A human readable, but non-parsable text, describing a filter's qualification.
* "tree": A tree data structure fully defining the filter's qualification.
* "tokens": A list of parsed tokens that define a filter's qualification.
Be aware that generating tokens requires additional time.

If omitted or `false`, only "text" and "tree" formats are returned.

If `true`, all "text", "tree" and "tokens" formats are returned.

ms-ExpressionRepresentation.name: showExpressionAs
ms-ExpressionRepresentation.description:
This parameter specifies the format in which the expressions are returned in response. 

This parameter applies to the expressions in a fact or attribute and custom expressions used in a filter, prompt, security filter, custom group, etc. 

Expressions are presented in the following formats:
* "text": A human readable, but non-parsable text, describing the expression.
  This is the default format that is always returned in the response.
* "tree": A tree data structure fully defining the expression.
  This format can be used if you want to examine and modify the expression programmatically.
* "tokens": A list of parsed tokens. 
  This format can be used if you want to examine and modify the expression using the parser component.
  Note that generating tokens requires additional time.
  
  Tokens are a semi-structured representation of MicroStrategy expression text that includes object references.
For example, let’s say a filter expression is "Revenue > Cost". When the filter expression is represented as tokens, the text is broken down into pieces (tokens) with information about what these pieces represent in the metadata: ("Revenue", Revenue_ID), (">", GreaterThan_ID), ("Cost", Cost_ID).

If omitted, the expression is returned in "text" format.

If `tree`, the expression is returned in "text" and "tree" formats.

If `tokens`, the expression is returned in "text" and "tokens" formats.

ms-CubeTemplateId.name: cubeTemplateId
ms-CubeTemplateId.description:
This parameter specifies a cube object that will be used as a template for new cube creation. The new cube will inherit the defintion of this cube, and can be overwritten through request body of POST /model/cubes.  

If ommitted, the new cube will be created based on a blank cube.

ms-ShowSqlPreview.name: showSqlPreview
ms-ShowSqlPreview.description:
Boolean value used to determine whether to show dynamic SQL preview or not.
Set to true by default.

If omitted or `false`, nothing will be returned for the advanced properties.
